notes.sourceDetail: Part II, Chapter I, Bahíyyih Khánum's spoken chronicle of Ásíyih Khánum, Bahá’u’lláh's imprisonment, and the family exile to Baghdad
notes.recentPressure: The Chosen Highway and bahai-library.com are warm again, and holy-family exile/imprisonment material now needs rest after Munirih, God Passes By, Haji Ramadan, and this Ásíyih Khánum selection. Fortitude, sacrifice, persecution, Baghdad, Bahíyyih narration, and monument-garden imagery should not become the next fallback.
underusedNext: non-bahai-library source if Clara-level
non-Persian or non-Holy-Land local community story with literary shape
women/local protagonists outside holy-family memoirs
fresh image lane not based on portraits or monument gardens
notes.searchCorrection: The Bahíyyih Khánum-focused version of the Chosen Highway passage was rejected as too close to the existing Greatest Holy Leaf story and portrait. Martha Root, Lidia/Vasyl, May Maxwell, and modern community candidates were interesting but either source-warm, report-like, fragmentary, or weaker on full verbatim source shape. Use Chosen Highway next only if the passage is extraordinary and clearly different.

notes.sourceDetail: Issue 527, Travelling institute fosters consolidation, Quilhuay, Peru, pp. 2-3
notes.recentPressure: Bahá’í News is now warm again, and community-consolidation/development-report material should not become the next fallback. The selection refreshed the immediate source mix after Memorials, Munirih memoir, Japan/Agnes Alexander, Portals, and Bahá’í World, but it adds Peru/Quechua, children/mothers classes, and local institute imagery to the recent pressure.
underusedNext: more literary memoir or book-length recollection outside report style
women or local protagonists with a complete personal arc
non-Bahá’í News source families
non-Peru/non-Quechua community material only if strongly story-shaped
notes.searchCorrection: Maharashtra/Vasantkutti from the same Bahá’í News issue was memorable and non-duplicate but had weaker image fit and a thinner source frame. Central African Republic women/manioc and India vocational-training passages met the underused-women target but read more like reports than Clara-level stories. Use modern/community-development material only when there is a concrete scene, human turn, and direct or very close image fit.

notes.recentPressure: Memorials of the Faithful is warm again despite the clean bahai.org host rotation. The new selection also adds devotion/teaching-under-persecution, learned Persian male protagonist, and direct portrait imagery, so these should rest next.
underusedNext: women/community transformation outside Memorials and holy-family memoirs
non-Persian or non-Western local community stories
non-bahai-library source families with narrative shape
periodical material only when it is concrete rather than report-like
notes.searchCorrection: Star of the West/Martha Root China, Czechoslovakia, and Australia pieces had source freshness but were too travel-report/informational for Clara today. Dorothy Baker Bahá’í News material had emotional force but weaker rights/source-shape for full verbatim body use. Nabíl-i-Akbar won because the official memorial was complete, literary, non-duplicative, and had a direct portrait.

notes.sourceDetail: pp. 38-51, Munirih Khanum's journey to ‘Akká, renaming, and marriage to ‘Abdu’l-Bahá
notes.recentPressure: Bahai-library.com has now supplied three consecutive additions, so source-host pressure is warm even though today's source family is fresh. Holy-family memoir, ‘Akká arrival, and dream-guidance material should rest after this selection; ‘Abdu’l-Bahá remains a frequent figure even though Munirih's voice leads the story.
underusedNext: non-bahai-library source if quality is high
non-‘Akká community transformation
women or local community stories outside holy-family memoirs
Promulgation or periodical material only if concrete and story-shaped
notes.searchCorrection: Khadijih Bagum's declaration recollection from the same memoir was rejected because it repeated the May 14 Dawn-Breakers declaration lane and House of the Báb image. The accepted Munirih passage used the Munírih Khánum portrait and avoided the recent Japan/first-believer, Portals/Bowery, and Bahá’í World/service patterns.

notes.sourceFamilyUsed: History of the Bahá'í Faith in Japan 1914-1938
notes.sourceDetail: chapter 3, Kikutaro Fukuta's first letter from Japan to ‘Abdu'l-Bahá, pp. 13-14
notes.recentPressure: Agnes Alexander/Japan is now newly used and should not become the next fallback. Awakening/first-believer material is warmer because this follows several recent recognition or transformation arcs, though it avoided the overused Bahá’í World, Portals, Memorials, and ‘Abdu’l-Bahá child/service lanes.
underusedNext: non-Japan Asian or Pacific community stories
women/community transformation outside first-convert framing
Covenant materials if rights-safe and self-contained
authorized memoir passages with fuller arcs and direct image fit
notes.searchCorrection: Keith Ransom-Kehler's Tihrán welcome had a strong image but repeated Star/Western visitor patterns; Bahiyyih Khanum exile material was powerful but echoed prison/persecution pressure. Fukuta's letter won because the source family was fresh, the passage was complete, and the image matched the named early Japanese believers.

notes.sourceDetail: chapter 4, Bowery boys / Black rose and Black sweet episode, pp. 60-68
notes.recentPressure: Portals to Freedom is no longer untouched and should rest for the next run. Unity and race-unity material are warmer after this New York child-centered scene, and ‘Abdu’l-Bahá remains a heavily recurring protagonist even though the source family was fresh.
underusedNext: non-‘Abdu’l-Bahá protagonists
women/community transformation outside Western pilgrimage or New York settings
non-Western community material from non-Bahá’í World sources
authorized memoirs with a different emotional register
notes.searchCorrection: The Bowery boys episode beat modern community-report candidates and merely talk-forward Promulgation material because it had concrete action, a complete emotional turn, exact source text, and a verified archival child/New York image. Avoid using Portals immediately as the new easy fallback.

notes.sourceDetail: Vol. 12, "Black Sunlight," Kampala young Buganda incident, pp. 926-927
notes.recentPressure: Bahá’í World is now overused/warm again after the new Black Sunlight selection and earlier Bahá’í World service/profile/remembrance pieces. Uganda/Kampala imagery is also warm because the new featured image is a 1961 Kampala House of Worship gathering.
underusedNext: non-Bahá’í World source families
women/community transformation outside modern report shape
authorized memoirs with direct image fit
concrete Promulgation action scenes only if story-shaped
notes.searchCorrection: DRC A Vision of Peace had strong freshness and image fit but read more like a modern development report; Barli Institute was more institutional than narrative. Black Sunlight won on literary voice, compact completeness, and memorability despite the Bahá’í World penalty.

notes.sourceDetail: chapter XVII, second commission and the ship that turned toward Constantinople
notes.recentPressure: God Passes By is now warm again after this Covenant-crisis/providence passage and the earlier Ridván declaration item. ‘Abdu’l-Bahá-centered danger in ‘Akká and prison imagery should rest for at least the next run.

notes.searchCorrection: Shamsu’d-Duhá from Memorials was the strongest runner-up but overlapped too much with the existing Ṭáhirih story on the same Memorials page. Bowery Mission remained too discourse-heavy and too close to charity/giving patterns.
